market_summary: S&P 500 gained +4.54% and Nasdaq +6.84% for the week of April 14-17, 2026, closing at all-time highs (S&P 7,041+). Nasdaq posted its 12th consecutive winning session — longest streak since 2009. CRITICAL NEW DEVELOPMENT: Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz as of approximately April 18-19, citing US breaches of trust — reversing the April 17 reopening that drove last week's equity rally. This reintroduces geopolitical risk premiums and materially strengthens the GLD thesis. AVGO surged +28% on AI chip partnerships with Meta (through 2029) and Google (through 2031). Dow gained +1.79% on Friday April 17.
macro_themes: Fed held at 3.50-3.75% for second consecutive meeting; FOMC April 28-29 widely expected to hold — JPMorgan now sees NO cuts in 2026 and a potential 25bps hike in Q3 2027 if inflation persists. March 2026 CPI confirmed +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM; core CPI +2.6% YoY. April CPI due May 12. Tariff passthrough expected to peak in Q2 2026 — rate cuts deferred to H2 2026 at earliest. Iran Hormuz re-closure is the dominant new macro development: oil supply risk restored, gold safe-haven bid reasserting, inflation expectations re-elevated. YTD 2026 sector rotation: Energy +21-34% YTD, Materials, Industrials leading over Technology — April saw counter-trend tech rally which may reverse with Hormuz re-closure.
portfolio_health: Grade: C+ (74/100). NAV $4,315.88. Week return +2.13% vs S&P 500 +4.54% = underperformance of -2.41pp, entirely attributable to 11.6% idle cash drag and CSPX structural underweight (26.2% actual vs ~36% target). CSPX ADD recommended for NINTH consecutive report without execution — singular operational failure of this portfolio. YTD since April 4 seed ($4,191): +2.98% vs CSPX YTD +3.16% — now marginally behind benchmark. All 7 positions positive unrealized. Iran Hormuz re-closure STRENGTHENS GLD thesis and partially restores U03A capital preservation value. ASML China mix fell to 19% (approaching 15% invalidation threshold — WATCH flag). MA earnings date corrected to April 30 (not April 23). WHT YTD: only $3.33 from MA dividend.

Subsequent steps: (1) conditional +$100 MA on April 30 earnings beat; (2) U03A stage-down -$100 post-FOMC April 28-29 if dovish signal received; (3) unconditional CSPX top-up by May 3 hard deadline with remaining cash. ASML monitored but not trimmed at current size due to commission inefficiency. Decisions driven purely by current thesis strength and market conditions — not anchored to prior report weights.
